channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [WSTCSTPAPR] WCPML Opens Special Window for Physical Share Transfer Requests
item.description:
West Coast Paper Mills Ltd has announced a special window for re-lodgement of transfer requests for physical shares, effective from February 05, 2026, to February 04, 2027. This initiative aligns with SEBI circulars, including the latest one dated January 30, 2026. The window is specifically for physical securities sold or purchased prior to April 01, 2019, and for any previous transfer requests that were rejected, returned, or not attended due to documentation or process deficiencies. Eligible shareholders are required to submit their original share certificates along with necessary documents to the company's Registrar and Transfer Agent (RTA), MUFG Intime India Pvt. Ltd. All share transfers under this window will be processed exclusively in dematerialized (demat) mode. The company published newspaper advertisements on April 17 and 18, 2026, to inform its shareholders.

  • WCPML opens special window for physical share transfers.
  • Window active from February 5, 2026, to February 4, 2027.
  • Covers shares sold/purchased before April 1, 2019.
  • Includes previously rejected or deficient transfer requests.
  • All share transfers will be processed only in demat mode.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: WEST COAST PAPER MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [NATFIT] National Fittings gets MCA nod to shift office to Gujarat
item.description:
National Fittings Ltd has received approval from the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) to shift its registered office from Tamil Nadu to Gujarat. The Regional Director, Chennai, issued a certificate of registration for the change of state on April 2, 2026. The new registered office will be located at 314/315, Village Mudhela, Taluka Savli, Vadodara - 391770, Gujarat. The company cited administrative efficiency and operational flexibility as reasons for the relocation. This move was reported pursuant to SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Registered office shifted from Tamil Nadu to Gujarat.
  • Ministry of Corporate Affairs approved the change on April 2, 2026.
  • New address: 314/315, Village Mudhela, Vadodara, Gujarat.
  • Relocation aims for administrative efficiency and operational flexibility.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: National Fittings Limited | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [INTERARCH] Interarch Building Solutions secures ₹60 Cr+ domestic order
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Interarch Building Solutions Ltd. has secured a new domestic order valued at approximately ₹60 Crores plus tax. The order encompasses the complete design, engineering, manufacturing, supply, and erection of a Pre-Engineered Steel Building System. The company expects to complete the project within an estimated period of 8 months. Due to confidentiality and commercial considerations, the name of the customer has not been disclosed. The company confirmed that there is no interest from the promoter/promoter group in the awarding entity, and the transaction does not fall under related party transactions.

  • Company secured a domestic order worth approx. ₹60 Crores plus tax.
  • Order involves design, engineering, manufacturing, supply & erection.
  • Project completion is estimated within 8 months.
  • Customer name remains confidential due to commercial reasons.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Interarch Building Products Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [HINDCOPPER] HCL reports fatality at Khetri plant; no operational disruption
item.description:
Hindustan Copper Ltd (HCL) reported an incident at its Khetri Copper Complex in Jhunjhunu, Rajasthan, on April 17, 2026. A contractual employee, engaged by a civil contractor, tragically died. The company expressed its condolences for the unfortunate event. Importantly, the filing confirmed that there was no disruption to the operations of the plant as a result of the incident.

  • Fatality reported at HCL's Khetri Copper Complex.
  • Incident involved one contractual employee on April 17, 2026.
  • Company confirmed no disruption to plant operations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HINDUSTAN COPPER LTD. | Category: Business Update

item.title: [KKSILK] K K Silk Mills clarifies share price movement is market-driven
item.description:
K K Silk Mills Ltd has issued a clarification regarding the movement in its share price, stating that the fluctuations are purely due to market conditions and are market-driven. The company emphasized that it has no control over these movements nor any knowledge of the underlying reasons. K K Silk Mills also reiterated its commitment to making timely disclosures of all material events and information as required under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, and assured that it will continue to keep the stock exchange informed of any such events.

  • K K Silk Mills attributes share price movement to market conditions.
  • Company states it has no control or knowledge of reasons for fluctuations.
  • Reaffirms timely disclosure of material events under SEBI regulations.
  • Will continue to inform stock exchanges of any required information.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: K K Silk Mills Ltd | Category: Clarification

item.title: [HARDWYN] Hardwyn secures 2,005 floor spring orders, launches ISI-certified products
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Hardwyn India Ltd has received purchase orders for a total of 2,005 floor springs from six prestigious government projects. These projects include the CM House in Chhattisgarh, Baba Raghav Das Medical College, UP Medical College in Gajipur, UP Medical College in Sidharth Nagar, UP Medical College in Pilibhit, and Chhatrapati Shahu Ji Maharaj University in Kanpur. Concurrently, Hardwyn has launched its new ISI-Certified floor springs, engineered for reliable glass door control and smooth closing action. These ISI-marked products are designed for commercial and high-traffic glass applications, emphasizing safety and durability, and are available in Silver and Black finishes.

  • Hardwyn secured orders for 2,005 floor springs from 6 government projects.
  • Key projects include CM House Chhattisgarh and multiple UP Medical Colleges.
  • Company launched new ISI-Certified floor springs for commercial use.
  • New products enhance safety and durability for high-traffic glass doors.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hardwyn India Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [HARDWYN] Hardwyn India Bags Orders for 2,005 Floor Springs from Govt Projects
item.description:
Hardwyn India Ltd has received purchase orders for a total of 2,005 floor springs from several prestigious government and institutional projects across Uttar Pradesh and Chhattisgarh. Key clients include CM House Chhattisgarh, Baba Raghav Das Medical College, multiple UP Medical Colleges, and Chhatrapati Shahu Ji Maharaj University. Concurrently, the company announced the launch of its new ISI-Certified floor springs, engineered for reliable glass door control and smooth closing action. These ISI-marked floor springs are designed for commercial and high-traffic glass applications, emphasizing safety and durability, and are available in Silver and Black finishes.

  • Hardwyn India secured orders for 2,005 floor springs.
  • Orders received from various government and institutional projects.
  • Company launched new ISI-certified floor springs.
  • New product targets commercial and high-traffic glass door applications.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hardwyn India Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [ENKEIWHEL] Enkei Wheels: 17th AGM Voting Results Out, All Resolutions Approved
item.description:
Enkei Wheels (India) Ltd announced the voting results of its 17th Annual General Meeting (AGM) held on April 16, 2026. Ms. Snehal Phirange, Company Secretary, served as the scrutinizer. Based on the consolidated report, all resolutions presented in the AGM notice were duly approved by the shareholders with the requisite majority. These resolutions included the adoption of the audited financial statements for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, the re-appointment of Mr. Junichi Suzuki as a Director, the increase in the company's borrowing limits under Section 180(1)(c) of the Companies Act, 2013, and the creation of charges on company properties for borrowings. The total number of shareholders on the record date (April 9, 2026) was 3,869. For each resolution, 14,620,291 votes were polled out of 17,974,895 outstanding shares, representing 81.3373% of polled votes, with 100% of polled votes in favour and 0% against.

  • Enkei Wheels announced voting results for its 17th AGM held on April 16, 2026.
  • All resolutions proposed at the AGM were approved by shareholders.
  • Resolutions included adopting FY2025 financials and re-appointing a director.
  • Shareholders approved increasing borrowing limits and creating charges on properties.
  • Total 81.34% of outstanding shares voted, with 100% in favor of all resolutions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ENKEI WHEELS (INDIA) LTD. | Category: AGM/EGM

item.title: [LUPIN] Lupin: US FDA inspection at Somerset facility concludes with 3 observations
item.description:
Lupin Ltd announced that the U.S. FDA concluded an inspection at its manufacturing facility in Somerset, New Jersey, U.S.A. The inspection, conducted from April 13 to April 17, 2026, resulted in the issuance of a Form-483 with three observations. The company stated its commitment to addressing these observations and responding to the U.S. FDA within the stipulated timeframe, reaffirming its dedication to CGMP standards across all its facilities.

  • US FDA inspected Lupin's Somerset, New Jersey facility.
  • Inspection concluded with three Form-483 observations.
  • Lupin will address observations and respond to FDA promptly.
  • Company is committed to CGMP standards across facilities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: LUPIN LTD. | Category: USFDA

item.title: [SAVINFOCO] Magnus Steel not a 'Large Corporate' as per SEBI norms
item.description:
Magnus Steel & Infra Limited has confirmed that it does not meet the criteria of a 'Large Corporate' as specified in Para 2.2 of SEBI Circular SEBI/HO/DDHS/CIR/P/2018/144, dated November 26, 2018. This circular outlines requirements for fund-raising by issuing debt securities by 'Large Entities' and related compliances. By not being classified as a 'Large Corporate', the company is exempt from specific compliance obligations applicable to such entities under the aforementioned SEBI circular.

  • Magnus Steel confirms it is not a 'Large Corporate'.
  • Classification is per SEBI Circular dated November 26, 2018.
  • Circular relates to fund-raising via debt securities.
  • Company is exempt from specific compliances for large entities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SAVANT INFOCOMM LTD. | Category: Regulatory
